steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The previous sentence establishes a specific location: "the wall of a university building in the United States." The blank opens the next sentence, which describes what the sculpture looks like at that spot. The transition must connect the location reference to the description that follows.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘There,’: "There" points back to the wall location named in the prior sentence and grounds the description in that physical place. It is a spatial reference that creates a direct, logical link. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘Later,’: "Later" signals a time shift. Nothing in the passage involves a time sequence; the description of the sculpture is not a later event. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘For instance,’: "For instance" introduces an example of something already stated. The prior sentence does not make a general claim that needs an example—it gives a specific location. Using "For instance" here is illogical. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘However,’: "However" signals contrast. The description of the sculpture as appearing to be two support beams does not contrast with the prior sentence—it elaborates on it. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
